interpretation: The synonymous-codon shuffler preserves ORF placement around the circular genome by construction (codons are permuted only within the original ORF window at the same nucleotide coordinates), and preserves the amino-acid translation of the ORF exactly because codons are swapped only among synonyms within the same amino-acid class. The audit confirms both properties on the n=500 obelisk subsample with confidently mapped Oblin-1 ORFs.
